title: Can music make your store feel more expensive? — Daniel Fox
meta_description: Customers can't always judge quality directly, so they read signals, and music is a strong one. Genre, volume, and brand fit each move how expensive a store feels and what people think your products are worth.
schema: <script type="application/ld+json">{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Article","headline":"Can music make your store feel more expensive?","description":"How music acts as a quality signal, raising perceived value, brand prestige, and purchase intent through affect transfer.","author":{"@type":"Person","name":"Daniel Christopher Fox","alternateName":"Daniel Fox","jobTitle":"Retail Music Behaviorist","url":"https://danielchristopherfox.com"},"publisher":{"@type":"Person","name":"Daniel Christopher Fox"},"mainEntityOfPage":"https://danielchristopherfox.com/notes/can-music-make-your-store-feel-premium.html","about":["Retail music","Perceived value","Brand prestige","Consumer behavior"]}</script>
